channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kerry Martin's Baby Mama Grieves After Child's Death
item.description: Faith Wanjiku, the baby mama of influencer Kerry Martin, has shared her heartbreak after losing her child. The 27-week premature birth led to severe medical complications, leaving Wanjiku with a KSh 5...

item.title: New York City Reports 23 Legionnaires' Disease Cases as Health Officials Investigate Upper East Side Outbreak
item.description: New York City health officials are investigating an outbreak of Legionnaires' disease centered in parts of the Upper East Side after the number of confirmed cases rose to 23 as of July 6. Seventeen pa...

item.title: Rising Grocery Prices Drive Canadians to Adopt 'Pay Later' Options
item.description: A growing number of Canadians are turning to 'pay later' payment options as grocery prices continue to climb, according to a report by an app that tracks consumer spending habits. The trend highlights...

item.title: Tanzania Police Strengthen Security Measures at Sabasaba Trade Fair
item.description: The Tanzania Police Force has assured the public that comprehensive security measures have been implemented at the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF) and across the country to ensure the sa...

item.title: South Africa's Path to Sustainable Energy Independence
item.description: The article highlights South Africa's proactive approach to building sustainable energy resilience, with the Western Cape leading the way towards achieving energy independence from Eskom by 2035. Key ...

item.title: Cotabato's Indigenous Crafts Sustained Through Government Initiatives
item.description: The provincial government of Cotabato, led by Governor Emmylou Taliño-Mendoza, has launched a program to sustain indigenous crafts and trades that provide income for marginalized communities. A recent...

item.title: Lessons from Norway's youth sports success for Australia
item.description: The article explores Norway's unique approach to youth sports, which emphasizes enjoyment over competition and has led to international success. Despite a population of just 5.6 million—about the size...

item.title: E-pop Band Koopa Passes Away at 46 Following Five-Year Recovery from Car Accident
item.description: The E-pop band Koopa, known for their vibrant music and cultural impact, has passed away at the age of 46. The tragedy occurred five years after a devastating car accident that left the group in criti...

item.title: Kyle Lowry Announces Retirement as Toronto Raptor After 9 Seasons
item.description: Kyle Lowry, the Canadian point guard who played nine seasons with the Toronto Raptors and was pivotal in their 2019 NBA championship run, has officially announced his retirement as a Raptor. The 40-ye...

item.title: Germany Sees Economic Benefits from Canada's Submarine Deal with TKMS
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil emphasized that Canada's selection of Germany's TKMS to build up to 12 submarines for its navy is a significant boost for Germany's...

item.title: KAMP Challenges KECOBO's Licence Suspension Over Embezzlement Allegations
item.description: The Kenya Association of Music Producers (KAMP) has strongly denied allegations of embezzlement by the Kenya Copyright Board (KECOBO), which suspended its operating licence on July 1, 2026. In a publi...

item.title: Brendan Canty criticises Vans' collaboration with Fugazi fashion brand as tone deaf
item.description: Brendan Canty, the drummer of the post-hardcore band Fugazi, has condemned a recent collaboration between Vans and a luxury streetwear brand named 'Fugazi' as 'tone deaf'. The footwear company initial...

item.title: Record-breaking El Nino expected to cause global weather extremes: expert
item.description: The current El Nino phenomenon is projected to break records for its intensity, according to Tim Stockdale, an expert at the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts. This extreme event coul...

item.title: South Africa's Path to Sustainable Energy Independence
item.description: The article highlights South Africa's efforts to build sustainable energy resilience, with the Western Cape leading the way in achieving energy independence from Eskom by 2035. Key strategies include ...

item.title: SIT Report Highlights 70 Cases of Donation Theft at Ayodhya Ram Temple; CJP’s X Account Restored
item.description: A Special Investigation Team (SIT) set up by Uttar Pradesh government to probe alleged embezzlement of donations at Ayodhya's Ram temple uncovered 70 instances of counting staff hiding cash in their c...

item.title: Nollywood Actress Cynthia Anijekwu Appeals for Help with Cancer Treatment
item.description: Popular Nollywood actress Cynthia Anijekwu has shared her struggle with cancer, appealing to Nigerians for financial support. Diagnosed in 2023, she underwent surgery and chemotherapy but faced a rela...

item.title: JD Vance's Memoir Criticized as Cult of Personality in Rolling Stone Article
item.description: Vice President JD Vance’s memoir, titled 'Communion,' faced a harsh critique in a Rolling Stone article by Stephen Rodrick. The piece argues that Vance’s book is not about personal faith but rather th...

item.title: Aristotle Viray and Co-Accused Face Legal Action in P75-M Plunder Case
item.description: PNP-CIDG Aristotle Baluyut Viray, one of Senator Rodante Marcoleta’s co-accused in his P75-million plunder case, has been detained at the New Quezon City Jail following his arrest. Viray, a businessma...

item.title: Day 2 of Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Threat Video Admitted, Prosecution Seeks Additional Witnesses
item.description: The second day of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial saw the prosecution present a 2024 video of Duterte threatening to kill President Ferdinand Marcos, Jr., First Lady Liza Marcos, and f...

item.title: Tragedy at Kapiti: Six Killed in Bus-Truck Collision Involving Tahmeed Express
item.description: Tahmeed Express, a Kenyan bus company, confirmed a fatal road accident in Kapiti, Konza that claimed six lives, including its driver and a truck driver. The crash occurred shortly after midnight, with...
